title: fix: Model Monitor scrolls as one page (no nested scrollbars)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes all nested scroll containers in the Model Monitor: the window is the only scroller (both axes)
- Drops the `polli-ui-shell` body class — it sets `body{overflow:hidden; height:100dvh}`, which forced the app into an inner-scroller shell
- Model column is the flexible one (`w-full min-w-[12rem] max-w-0` + truncate): on desktop the table fits the page exactly; on narrow screens the whole page pans right and the card grows with the table
- **packages/ui**: removes `overflow-x: hidden` from `html.polli-ui-root` — audited every consumer (enter dashboard swaps to shell dynamically; enter document-flow routes use `polli-ui-body` which self-clips; playground/websim never set `polli-ui-root`; pollinations.ai doesn't use these classes) — no consumer relies on the html-level clip
- Reqs column drops the "(+4xx)" bracket totals (the 4xx % column carries that); Speed column header is now "tok/s" so rows show bare numbers
- Verified with Playwright at 375/900/1200/1440px: zero inner scrollers, no clipping, truncation and tok/s sorting work

Preview deploy: https://fix-monitor-double-scroll.apps-model-monitor-52l.pages.dev

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: One scroll to rule them
gist.blurb: The Model Monitor has stopped nesting tiny scroll universes inside each other. Polly swept out the extra containers, so the page now moves like a page.
gist.summary: Model Monitor now uses the browser window as its only vertical and horizontal scroller, with a flexible truncating model column, page-level narrow-screen panning, simplified Reqs labels, and tok/s speed headers.
gist.impact: Users can scroll and sort the Model Monitor cleanly at 375px through desktop widths without clipped tables or competing nested scrollbars.
gist.visual_concept: Depict the Model Monitor as one cozy, wide retro terminal table extending naturally across a garden workbench, rather than several boxes trapped inside one another. Polly, wearing a tiny janitor scarf and holding a broom, clears away small discarded scrollbars while a code-plant vine grows along the single page path; the monitor robot can happily point at the clean tok/s column.
gist.image_prompt: A cozy pixel art garden hackerspace where a single wide CRT Model Monitor table scrolls smoothly across a warm cream and prominently lime green (#ecf874) page, while tiny discarded nested scrollbar boxes sit in a tidy compost pile beside code-plants and flowers. The main character is a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings; she wears a tiny janitor scarf and sweeps the extra scrollbars away as a chunky CRT robot points at a glowing "tok/s" header. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle scanlines, emotionally warm.
